title: Cottage Pie

overview.description: A classic British pie made with slow-cooked beef in a rich gravy, topped with buttery mashed potato and baked until crisp and golden. A comforting family meal that freezes beautifully.

method: Peel the potatoes, cut into chunks and cook in salted boiling water until tender.
Meanwhile, heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan and brown the beef mince thoroughly.
Add the onions, carrots and celery and cook for around 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Sprinkle over the flour and stir well.
Gradually add the beef stock, followed by the Worcestershire sauce, thyme and bay leaf.
Simmer gently for 35–45 minutes until the filling has thickened.
Stir through the frozen peas during the final 5 minutes of cooking.
Drain the potatoes and mash with the butter and milk until smooth. Season well.
Remove the bay leaf and transfer the filling to a large ovenproof dish.
Spread the mashed potato evenly over the filling, creating ridges with a fork.
Bake at 200°C (180°C fan) for 30 minutes until the topping is crisp and golden.
Leave to rest for 10 minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: The name 'Cottage Pie' dates back to the late 18th century, when potatoes became an affordable staple in rural Britain. It originally referred specifically to a pie made with beef.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a rich beef filling, balance gravy consistency and create a crisp, golden mashed potato topping.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An economical family classic that's ideal for filling the freezer with homemade meals. It makes excellent use of beef mince while staying simple and comforting.
chapman-tips: Allow the filling to become properly thick before adding the mashed potato.
Adding the peas near the end keeps their colour and texture.
Creating ridges in the mash gives more crispy golden edges after baking.
A splash of leftover beef cooking liquor from the Jamie Oliver Beef Short Ribs makes an exceptional Cottage Pie.
notes.batch-cooking: Bake two pies while the oven is on and freeze one whole or in portions for up to three months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat thoroughly until piping hot. Great for lunches the following day.
